svg.text: 6 levels of the classic ICS/OT hierarchy · OTLab component coverage mapped to each level

g.text: ERP / CRM / email / business apps — managed by corporate IT, never directly touches OT
MES, scheduling, work order, file servers — site-level IT that knows about manufacturing
OTLab V4.1: corp AD · file server · jump host (lives in ent-br0 192.168.50/24)
DMZ — only allowed path between IT and OT zones
Reverse proxies, jump hosts, Historian replica, AV servers · firewalls enforce IT↛L3, OT↛L4, DMZ↔both
OTLab: dashboard (.40) · Authentik · Guacamole · Ignition · CODESYS [in dmz-br0 192.168.75/24]
SCADA servers, operator stations, Historian, batch management, alarm/event aggregation
OTLab: OpenPLC web UIs (:8081/:8082) · Ignition SCADA (V3.0) · Suricata IDS classroom aggregation
In classroom mode: teacher SIEM (Loki + Grafana) aggregates per-student Suricata into one view
Local HMIs, area engineering workstations, line-of-sight supervisory PCs, batch sequencers
OTLab: OpenPLC web UIs as operator HMI · sensor-sim control HTTP (:5021) for scenario injection
PLCs (Allen-Bradley, Siemens, Schneider…) · RTUs · IEDs · running ladder logic / IEC 61131-3 / function blocks
OTLab: virtual OpenPLC #1 (master, .60) · OpenPLC #2 (outstation, .61) · modbus-master (.43, 100ms poll) · DNP3 outstation (.71)
OTLab physical: Raspberry Pi running OpenPLC v3 with real GPIO (Phase 2 — l1-plc-01)
Conpot honeypot personas (Siemens / Schneider / Rockwell) — deception layer mimicking real L1 devices
The physical process — what L1 PLCs read from and write to
Temperature sensors, pressure transmitters, flow meters, motors, valves, level switches, VFDs
OTLab: sensor-sim (.70) simulates a water-treatment scenario · provides Modbus FC1-6 + control HTTP
OTLab physical (Phase 3): RS485 Modbus sensors via Waveshare gateway · ESP32 wireless IoT
L3 Site Ops — Loki/Grafana classroom SIEM
L2 HMI surface — OpenPLC web UIs
L1 PLCs — 2× virt OpenPLC + modbus-master + DNP3
L4 Enterprise zone — corp AD + file server (V4.1)
L3.5 DMZ — Authentik IdP + Guacamole jump (V4.2)
L3.5 DMZ — Ignition Maker SCADA + CODESYS (V3.0+)
L3.5 DMZ — VyOS firewall replaces fw-dmz-pcn (V4.3)
L0 Sensors — RS485 + ESP32 physical gear (Phase 3)
L5 Enterprise cloud — never the lab focus
L4 ERP / CRM — bigger than a teaching lab
— but the boundaries (DMZ, AD trust, segmentation)
are taught via the L3.5 + V4.1 work
· Level color = Purdue convention (cool top → warm bottom)
The Purdue Enterprise Reference Architecture (PERA) — Theodore J. Williams, 1989. Updated for ISA-99/IEC 62443 in 2000s.
OTLab teaches L0-L3.5 in depth (L4 in V4.1) — the OT zones where ICS security actually matters.
